trivialization_signals_checked: Does the analog reduce yadṛcchayā to luck-talk? No — the verse explicitly distinguishes unsought (yadṛcchayā) from lucky-feeling.

Does the analog flatten svarga-dvāra to 'good career move'? No — the engineering layer maps svarga-dvāra to 'gate has opened' which preserves the directional-walk-through framing without smuggling in metaphysical-heaven claims.
Does the analog reduce sukhinaḥ to 'be grateful'? No — sukhinaḥ is rendered as descriptive of the fortunate-state, not prescriptive of the desired emotion.
Does the analog drop the kṣatriya-specificity? No — preserved via role-alignment scoping.
verdict_reason: The doctrinal weight is preserved at every layer; the source pack carries traditional_meaning_consensus naming the unsought-ness as load-bearing; the engineering layer is additive. No trivialisation. PASS.
